title: helianthemum polifolium subsp. apenninum
intro: Helianthemum polifolium subsp. apenninum, also known as Rockrose, is a flowering plant species native to the Apennine Mountains in Italy. It is a low-growing, woody shrub with pink or white flowers that have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids, terpenes, and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Rockrose include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of conditions, from skin irritations and wounds to respiratory issues and digestive problems.
